governing_fact: Every Tesserine surface is a typeset record: the quiet, documentary projection of a machine-readable artifact that remains the product. (design/v1/SYSTEM.md)

members.role: the ground every surface sits on
members.derivation: Entailment 1 (a document's ground): a record is read like paper, so the ground is warm paper — not screen-white glare — and disappears under the record.
Entailment 1 (a document's ground): warm near-black ink on warm paper, the documentary pair; contrast far above sustained-reading need without the harshness of pure black on pure white.

members.role: secondary matter that recedes: metadata, captions, provenance
members.derivation: Entailment 1 (a document's ground): a record's secondary matter recedes without dropping legibility — one muted step of the same warm ink, still comfortably readable at caption size.
Entailment 2 (one rhythm, one ratio): structure is marked by the least sufficient rule, so the rule ink sits at the lowest contrast that still delineates — structure shown, never shouted.
Entailment 3 (ink speaks only for the record): a citation is the record speaking — the one non-state departure from ink, set in annotation blue-black like a ledger's cross-reference.

members.role: a state the record reports as passing or healthy
members.derivation: Entailment 3 (ink speaks only for the record): the record's own passing state, in a deep documentary green legible as text on the paper ground.

members.role: a state the record reports as needing attention
members.derivation: Entailment 3 (ink speaks only for the record): the record's own needs-attention state, in a dark amber that stays legible as text rather than bright warning paint.

members.role: a state the record reports as failing
members.derivation: Entailment 3 (ink speaks only for the record): the record's own failing state, in a deep red that reads as ink, not alarm decoration.
Entailment 2 (one rhythm, one ratio): a quarter of the reading line (1.5rem), the smallest named interval on the rhythm.

members.role: space between grouped elements inside a form
members.derivation: Entailment 2 (one rhythm, one ratio): half of the reading line (1.5rem); grouping distance on the rhythm.

members.role: space between blocks of running matter
members.derivation: Entailment 2 (one rhythm, one ratio): the reading line itself — the body step's 1rem at its 1.5 line height — the unit every other space is named from.
Entailment 2 (one rhythm, one ratio): two reading lines; a section boundary is a full breath on the same rhythm, not an arbitrary gap.
Entailment 2 (one rhythm, one ratio): the least sufficient mark — the thinnest rule a screen draws — because a record delineates, it does not decorate.

members.role: line length of the reading column
members.derivation: Entailment 1 (a document's ground): the book measure for sustained reading, ~66 characters — the record is read at length, so the column is set like a book's.
Entailment 2 (one rhythm, one ratio): running matter carries the reading weight; only structure departs from it.

members.role: text that ranks the record: titles and headings
members.derivation: Entailment 2 (one rhythm, one ratio): structure is marked by weight rather than ornament — one bold step against the reading weight, nothing between.

members.value: Georgia, 'Iowan Old Style', 'Times New Roman', serif
members.role: face for everything read as prose
members.derivation: Entailment 1 (a document's ground): a typeset record is set in a book serif, and in stock faces only — the projection renders without fetching a display face, so the ground stays quiet everywhere the record travels.
Entailment 3 (ink speaks only for the record): the record's verbatim matter stays visibly machine-shaped — a stock monospace, so what the machine said is never dressed as prose.
